patch.value: pm schema add-status: register custom statuses (complement to add-type)
schema/statuses.json is loaded at runtime but there is no add-status command; users hand-edit JSON to add review/pending_approval/wont_fix.
pm schema add-status <id> --role <active|blocked|terminal_done|terminal_canceled> --alias --description writes statuses.json; role-validated; idempotent; surfaced by schema list; MCP+contracts+tests.
schema/statuses.json is loaded at runtime but there is no add-status command; users hand-edit JSON to add review/pending_approval/wont_fix.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented pm schema add-status/remove-status. Statuses PERSIST in .agents/pm/schema/statuses.json (shape {statuses: RuntimeStatusDefinition[]}), NEW lock id 'schema-statuses'. New core module src/core/schema/status-defs-file.ts (mirrors item-types-file.ts): parseStatusDefsFile/serializeStatusDefsFile/normalizeAddStatusInput (roles validated vs RUNTIME_STATUS_ROLE_VALUES; id normalized lowercase + spaces/hyphens->underscore matching runtime-schema.ts)/upsertStatusDef (idempotent by normalized id, replaced:true on re-add)/removeStatusDef + BUILTIN_STATUS_IDS. When file absent, start from [] (runtime merge layer supplies defaults from DEFAULT_RUNTIME_STATUS_DEFINITIONS; init scaffolds it). BUILTIN_STATUS_IDS derived from DEFAULT_RUNTIME_STATUS_DEFINITIONS = draft/open/in_progress/blocked/closed/canceled (the spec's 5 plus draft); remove-status refuses all of them. items_using_status:<N> warning counts ALL items whose resolved status (via alias_to_id) matches, via listAllFrontMatterLight, non-blocking. schema list extended with statuses section (builtin vs custom, id/roles/aliases) via resolveRuntimeStatusRegistry. SCHEMA-VERSION BUMP: yes, x-schema-version 4.0.1->4.0.2 (new subcommand enum + role/order params); updated contracts-command + sdk-index specs (no .snap snapshots exist). --role is repeatable Commander collect (list:false), --order numeric. 100% core coverage; all 2167 tests green.

patch.value: Added pm schema add-status <id> and pm schema remove-status <id> writing schema/statuses.json under a new schema-statuses lock. New core module status-defs-file.ts (parse/serialize/normalizeAddStatusInput/upsertStatusDef/removeStatusDef/BUILTIN_STATUS_IDS), 100% covered. Roles validated vs RUNTIME_STATUS_ROLE_VALUES; idempotent upsert (replaced:true); remove-status refuses the 6 built-in defaults + warns items_using_status:<N>. schema list now surfaces a statuses section (builtin vs custom). Wired MCP+contracts+completion+docs.
pm schema add-status/remove-status manage custom statuses; surfaced by schema list; role-validated and idempotent.
Verified in temp dir: add-status review (Registered then Updated on re-add), invalid role rejected, remove-status open refused, schema list shows custom statuses. Full coverage gate green.

Delivered pm schema add-status/remove-status (schema/statuses.json, schema-statuses lock, role-validated, idempotent, refuses built-in statuses, schema list surfaces statuses section). New status-defs-file.ts core helper 100% covered; MCP+contracts+completion+docs+tests. Verified in temp dir. Part of schema-customization-completion PR (plan pm-z5nb).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: PR #106 review remediation: fixed a data-loss bug in add-status. Omitting --role/--alias on re-add no longer wipes existing roles/aliases. NormalizedAddStatusInput.roles/aliases are now optional; normalizeAddStatusInput returns undefined when the raw field was not supplied (omitted = preserve) vs an explicit empty array (clear); upsertStatusDef only touches roles/aliases when the field is defined. Also hardened --order validation on both the CLI (register-mutation parseSchemaOrderOption) and MCP (server.ts schema action) paths: a present-but-non-finite order now throws a usage error instead of being silently dropped. Tests added in tests/unit/item-types-file.spec.ts (omitted-preserves, explicit-empty-clears, mixed clear). Full coverage gate 100%, static gate green.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review round-2 (Codex) hardening: (1) add-status now rejects id/alias collisions with a DIFFERENT existing status (assertStatusTokensAvailable vs the resolved alias_to_id) so a custom status can't shadow a built-in lifecycle token e.g. --alias open; (2) settings-defined statuses are preserved on upsert — upsertStatusDef seeds from the resolved (settings-or-file) definition via baseDefinition so omitting --role/--alias no longer drops roles that live in settings.schema.statuses rather than statuses.json; (3) remove-status now warns status_referenced_by_workflow:<slot> when a workflow default still points at the removed status; (4) --order rejects fractional/non-integer values on both CLI and MCP paths. New assertStatusTokensAvailable + baseDefinition branch 100% covered.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review round-3 (Codex) hardening: (C5) add-status collision check is now ALSO re-run under the schema-statuses lock against the freshly-read statuses.json, closing a TOCTOU where two concurrent add-status could both pass the pre-lock check and write colliding ids/aliases; (C6) add-status now REJECTS built-in status ids (open/in_progress/blocked/closed/canceled/draft) in normalizeAddStatusInput — symmetric with remove-status refusing them — so a reserved status can't be overridden into an un-removable state. Tests added (built-in rejection, collision helper). Full coverage gate 100%.
